cataloging_subject.label: Younger Supplementary Legends from MS Bodley 779 (Horstmann selection)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged subject is Carl Horstmann's exact selection of thirty-three anonymous Middle English verse legends from Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 779, printed in two 1889 journal installments and joined by the CME encoding. It is not the complete South English Legendary as copied in Bodley 779 and not one stable authorial work. The selection omits intervening manuscript items; several later selected legends occupy writing zones alongside Barlaam and Josaphat.

catalog_summary.title: Younger Supplementary Legends from MS Bodley 779 (Horstmann selection)
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous
catalog_summary.date: Selected manuscript forms first attested in the second quarter of the fifteenth century (1425–1450); constituent composition dates vary and remain unresolved here
catalog_summary.region: North Hampshire, England, for Bodley 779 compiler orthography; the places of composition of the selected legends are not established

titles.value: Younger Supplementary Legends from MS Bodley 779 (Horstmann selection)

assertion.notes: An English supplied title that preserves the edition's manuscript and selection boundary.

titles.value: Des Ms. Bodl. 779 jüngere Zusatzlegenden zur südlichen Legendensammlung

agents.name: Anonymous authors of the selected legends
notes: No secure named authorship is assigned to the thirty-three selected texts.

scope.description: Compilation and arrangement in the sole manuscript witness, not authorship of every constituent legend

date_statements.display: Second quarter of the fifteenth century (1425–1450)

assertion.notes: Bodley 779 is the sole witness claimed for this selected group or these versions; DIMEV records the individual texts separately.

notes: The statement applies to the exact selected manuscript forms, not necessarily to every underlying legend tradition.
The selection appeared in two installments in the same journal volume.

assertion.notes: Görlach localizes the compiler's orthographic modification of source texts; this does not prove the selected legends were composed there.

notes: Kept separate from place of composition and from physical manuscript production.

form_statements.description: All thirty-three selected legends are represented as verse; the XML contains 5,182 L elements grouped in 117 LG elements.

summaries.value: An edited selection of thirty-three Middle English verse legends from the idiosyncratic fifteenth-century South English Legendary compilation in Bodley 779. The sequence opens with the Feast of Corpus Christi and includes lives of friars, hermits, bishops, martyrs, paired saints, English saints, and an especially dense group of popes. Horstmann presented these manuscript-specific additions or versions in two 1889 installments; the selection preserves neither the manuscript's full contents nor its continuous codex order.

Thirty-three verse legends totaling 5,182 encoded lines. Twelve appear on printed pp. 307–353 and twenty-one on pp. 369–422. The omitted printed pp. 354–368 are unrelated journal matter, and excluded manuscript legends intervene between many selected items.

extent.description: Thirty-three DIV2 legend units; 5,182 XML L elements in 117 LG groups. The groups are encoding structures and are not asserted as canonical stanza counts.

content_parts.label: Feast of Corpus Christi
content_parts.summary: Verse legend and doctrinal account of the Corpus Christi feast.
notes: DIMEV 5329 indexes both Bodley 779 and British Library Egerton MS 2810, ff. 176v–179v; Horstmann's sole-witness claim therefore does not hold for this individual legend.

content_parts.label: Life of St Francis
content_parts.summary: Verse life of Saint Francis of Assisi.
notes: DIMEV 5517; the manuscript ending is imperfect and the remainder of fol. 183r is blank.

content_parts.label: Life of St Faith
content_parts.summary: Verse life and martyrdom of Saint Faith.

content_parts.label: Life of St Nycasie
content_parts.summary: Verse life and martyrdom of Saint Nicasius.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Calixtus
content_parts.summary: Verse papal life of Calixtus I.

content_parts.label: Life of St Winifred
content_parts.summary: Verse life and martyrdom of the Welsh virgin Saint Winifred.

content_parts.label: Life of St Hilarion
content_parts.summary: Verse life of the hermit Saint Hilarion.

content_parts.label: Lives of Saints Chrysanthus and Daria
content_parts.summary: Paired verse martyr legend of Chrysanthus and Daria.

content_parts.label: Lives of Saints Crispin and Crispinian
content_parts.summary: Paired verse martyr legend of Crispin and Crispinian.

content_parts.label: Life of St Evarist
content_parts.summary: Verse papal life of Evaristus.

content_parts.label: Life of St Firmin
content_parts.summary: Verse life of the bishop Saint Firmin.

content_parts.label: Life of St Vital
content_parts.summary: Verse martyr legend of Saint Vitalis.

content_parts.label: Life of St Oswin
content_parts.summary: Verse life and martyrdom of the Northumbrian king Saint Oswin.

content_parts.label: Lives of Saints John and Paul
content_parts.summary: Paired verse martyr legend of the Roman brothers John and Paul.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Leo II
content_parts.summary: Short verse papal life of Leo II.

content_parts.label: Life of St Marius and His Family
content_parts.summary: Verse martyr legend of Marius, Martha, Audifax, and Abachum.

content_parts.label: Life of St Illurin (Silverius)
content_parts.summary: Verse papal martyr legend identified by the catalog with Silverius.

content_parts.label: Life of St Paulin (or Paul) the Hermit
content_parts.summary: Verse eremitic life of Paulin or Paul the Hermit.
notes: DIMEV 4257; the catalog reports the final ten manuscript lines on fol. 227r as crossed out.

content_parts.label: Life of St Sylvester
content_parts.summary: Verse papal life of Sylvester I.
notes: DIMEV 555; Barlaam and Josaphat begins on fol. 228v, so the works overlap spatially on later surfaces.

content_parts.label: Life of St Remigius
content_parts.summary: Verse episcopal life of Saint Remigius.
notes: DIMEV 5333; the catalog's end locus is inferred. Visual inspection places the opening partway down Digital Bodleian's fol. 229av canvas alongside Barlaam and Josaphat.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Anicetus
content_parts.summary: Short verse papal life of Anicetus.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Soter
content_parts.summary: Short verse papal life of Soter.
notes: DIMEV 5010; an XML milestone reads 182 where the manuscript and catalog support fol. 232.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Caius
content_parts.summary: Short verse papal life of Caius.

content_parts.label: Life of St Emerentiana
content_parts.summary: Short verse virgin-martyr legend of Emerentiana.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Hyginus
content_parts.summary: Short verse papal life of Hyginus.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Miltiades
content_parts.summary: Short verse papal life of Miltiades.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Damasus
content_parts.summary: Short verse papal life of Damasus I.

content_parts.label: Life of St Saturninus
content_parts.summary: Verse martyr legend of Saint Saturninus.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Innocent I
content_parts.summary: Short verse papal life of Innocent I.

content_parts.label: Life of Pope Felix III
content_parts.summary: Short verse papal life of Felix III.

content_parts.label: Lives of Saints Simplice and Faustine
content_parts.summary: Paired verse martyr legend of Simplicius and Faustinus.

content_parts.label: Lives of Saints Abdon and Ceman
content_parts.summary: Paired verse martyr legend of Abdon and Sennen, named Ceman in the manuscript tradition.

content_parts.label: Life of St Germanus
content_parts.summary: Verse episcopal life and miracle narrative of Saint Germanus of Auxerre.
notes: DIMEV 4604; visual inspection shows Germanus begins at the top of canvas 496 (fol. 240v) and ends in the upper portion of canvas 498 (provider label fol. 241av), where the Life of St Aethelwold begins below.

evidence.summary: Confirms the selection title, Horstmann byline, thirty-three legend divisions, text, notes, printed page breaks, manuscript folio milestones, and encoded language.
Counted 33 DIV2 legends, 5,182 L elements, 117 LG elements, 101 PB elements, and two non-contiguous printed runs; calculated part line counts directly from the XML.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for Horstmann's 1889 edition.
evidence.summary: Supports the two-installment edition boundary, titles, editor, manuscript sources, folio mappings, and text descriptions.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for the official Bodley 779 catalog record.
evidence.summary: Supports shelfmark, date, origin, dialect note, contents and loci, DIMEV cross-references, codicology, provenance, and facsimile status.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for DIMEV version 1.0.
evidence.summary: Supports individual text identities, incipits, witness loci, and edition references for all thirty-three parts.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for Kanno's 2016 Bodley 779 thesis.
evidence.summary: Supports plural and fluid South English Legendaries modeling and the manuscript's date, dialect, idiosyncratic compilation, unique saints, and papal-life grouping.

evidence.citation: Complete Digital Bodleian IIIF bundle audit and visual boundary inspection, 2026-07-12.
evidence.locator: 672 canvases; target at one-based bundle inventory positions 357–498 in discontiguous runs
evidence.summary: Validated ZIP integrity, inventoried every canvas, mapped selected folios, and visually confirmed shared or spatial boundaries at Corpus Christi, Remigius beside Barlaam and Josaphat, and Germanus before Aethelwold.

open_questions.question: Which individual selected versions can be dated or localized more narrowly than their second-quarter-fifteenth-century witness?
next_steps: Review DIMEV record bibliographies and current scholarship for each constituent text.
notes: Do not convert the manuscript compiler's Hampshire orthography into a place-of-composition claim.

open_questions.question: Can the Bodleian supply a formal concordance between the catalog's Germanus locus fols. 240v–241v and the current Digital Bodleian labels fol. 240v through fol. 241av?
next_steps: Ask the Bodleian cataloging team only if a formal foliation crosswalk becomes necessary; retain both current claims meanwhile.

notes: Horstmann's claim that Bodley 779 is the only manuscript is preserved as an edition-era claim about the selected group or versions; the thirty-three DIMEV records remain the safer unit for individual witness censuses.
DIMEV 1.0 gives a second Corpus Christi witness in British Library Egerton MS 2810, ff. 176v–179v; each of the other thirty-two selected records has one indexed witness in that release.
The preferred English title is supplied for discoverability and explicitly retains the edited-selection boundary.
The XML milestone in the Soter legend reading 182 is treated as a source/encoding typo because the official catalog and physical sequence support fol. 232.
